old_claim.note: Humanism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Renaissance humanist philosophy and revived classical learning supplied cultural context for naturalistic representation, including perspective, without being a single technical requirement.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica frames Renaissance art with humanist philosophy, revived classical learning, naturalism, and formalized linear perspective.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source links Renaissance humanism and classical revival to the artistic setting in which perspective was formalized.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Humanism remains enabling rather than required because it explains the cultural program around naturalistic representation without supplying the geometric construction itself.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: perspective_in_art keeps humanism as an enabling cultural-context edge with source support.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to the geometric construction alone.
The edge is expected to represent a hard technical dependency rather than cultural context.
A stronger source separates humanist philosophy from perspective adoption in Florence.
short_reason: Humanism is sourced cultural context for Renaissance perspective.
